### Type Check Type Reference - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/typescript/blob/main/tests/baselines/reference/parserRealSource11.errors.txt

Performs type checking on type reference nodes by resolving the type link and validating the term. Manages type checking state and prevents void constructors, returning the checked AST node.

```typescript
public typeCheck(typeFlow: TypeFlow) {
  var prevInTCTR = typeFlow.inTypeRefTypeCheck;
  typeFlow.inTypeRefTypeCheck = true;
  var typeLink = getTypeLink(this, typeFlow.checker, true);
  typeFlow.checker.resolveTypeLink(typeFlow.scope, typeLink, false);
  if (this.term) {
    typeFlow.typeCheck(this.term);
  }
  typeFlow.checkForVoidConstructor(typeLink.type, this);
  this.type = typeLink.type;
  if (this.term) {
    this.term.type = this.type;
  }
  typeFlow.inTypeRefTypeCheck = prevInTCTR;
  return this;
}
```

### TypeScript Bivariant Type Hack Callback Validation

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/typescript/blob/main/tests/baselines/reference/strictFunctionTypesErrors.errors.txt

Illustrates how TypeScript handles callback type checking with bivariant type hacks using conditional types. The BivariantHack type extracts method signatures from object properties, which can exhibit bivariant behavior. Despite using this type manipulation technique, strict function type checking still enforces contravariance rules for callback parameters, preventing incompatible function assignments.

```typescript
namespace n2 {
    type BivariantHack<Input, Output> = { foo(x: Input): Output }["foo"];
    declare let f1: (cb: BivariantHack<Animal, Animal>) => void;
    declare let f2: (cb: BivariantHack<Dog, Animal>) => void;
    f1 = f2;
    f2 = f1;  // Error
    // TS2322: Type '(cb: (x: Animal) => Animal) => void' is not assignable to type '(cb: (x: Dog) => Animal) => void'.
}
```

### Truthiness Check as Type Guard in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/typescript/blob/main/tests/baselines/reference/typeGuardsAsAssertions.errors.txt

The `f1` function illustrates how a simple truthiness check (`if (x)`) can act as a type guard. If `x` is truthy (not `undefined`, `null`, `0`, `false`, `''`), its type is narrowed within the `if` block. Outside the block, the type reverts to its original union.

```typescript
function f1() {
    let x: string | number | undefined = undefined;
    x;  // undefined
    if (x) {
        x;  // string | number (guard as assertion)
    }
    x;  // string | number | undefined
}
```

```typescript
public typeCheck(typeFlow: TypeFlow) {
```

### Type Check Switch Statement (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/typescript/blob/main/tests/baselines/reference/parserRealSource11.errors.txt

This method performs type checking for a `switch` statement. It type-checks the main expression of the `switch` statement (`this.val`), iterates through and type-checks all expressions within the `caseList`, and finally type-checks the `defaultCase`. It uses a `TypeFlow` object to manage the type-checking process and updates the statement's type to `voidType`.

```typescript
public typeCheck(typeFlow: TypeFlow) {
    var len = this.caseList.members.length;
    this.val = typeFlow.typeCheck(this.val);
    for (var i = 0; i < len; i++) {
        this.caseList.members[i] = typeFlow.typeCheck(this.caseList.members[i]);
    }
    this.defaultCase = <CaseStatement>typeFlow.typeCheck(this.defaultCase);
    this.type = typeFlow.voidType;
    return this;
}
```
